description: Norovirus is a highly contagious virus and a leading cause of acute gastroenteritis and foodborne disease outbreaks worldwide, transmitted primarily through person-to-person contact and contaminated food and water.

llm_entry.description: Norovirus is a highly contagious virus and a leading cause of acute gastroenteritis and foodborne disease outbreaks worldwide, transmitted primarily through person-to-person contact and contaminated food and water.
llm_entry.name: Norovirus
llm_entry.notes: Evidence count: 7711 articles across PubMed, Europe PMC, PubTator Central, and CDC. Transmission occurs primarily person-to-person, but foodborne outbreaks are well-documented, particularly via fresh produce and shellfish. Increasing concern due to rising infections in immunocompromised populations.

name: Norovirus
notes: Evidence count: 7711 articles across PubMed, Europe PMC, PubTator Central, and CDC. Transmission occurs primarily person-to-person, but foodborne outbreaks are well-documented, particularly via fresh produce and shellfish. Increasing concern due to rising infections in immunocompromised populations.

description: Bacillus cereus is a spore-forming bacterium that can cause food poisoning through emetic and diarrheal syndromes, producing the toxin cereulide.

llm_entry.description: Bacillus cereus is a spore-forming bacterium that can cause food poisoning through emetic and diarrheal syndromes, producing the toxin cereulide.
llm_entry.name: Bacillus cereus
llm_entry.notes: Evidence supported by PubTator Central annotations (74 total) and CDC outbreak data. Produces two distinct toxin syndromes: emetic (cereulide) and diarrheal. Spore-forming nature allows survival of cooking temperatures.

name: Bacillus cereus
notes: Evidence supported by PubTator Central annotations (74 total) and CDC outbreak data. Produces two distinct toxin syndromes: emetic (cereulide) and diarrheal. Spore-forming nature allows survival of cooking temperatures.

description: 食品包装材料中的化学品迁移至食品中，主要包括邻苯二甲酸酯等增塑剂和内分泌干扰物，可对生殖健康和胎儿发育产生不良影响。

llm_entry.description: 食品包装材料中的化学品迁移至食品中，主要包括邻苯二甲酸酯等增塑剂和内分泌干扰物，可对生殖健康和胎儿发育产生不良影响。

description: Brucella is a genus of gram-negative bacteria that causes brucellosis, a zoonotic disease transmitted to humans through consumption of unpasteurized dairy products or contact with infected animals.

llm_entry.description: Brucella is a genus of gram-negative bacteria that causes brucellosis, a zoonotic disease transmitted to humans through consumption of unpasteurized dairy products or contact with infected animals.
llm_entry.name: Brucella
llm_entry.notes: Main routes of infection are consumption of raw (unpasteurized) dairy products from goats, sheep, or cattle. Also known as Malta fever, Mediterranean fever, or undulant fever. Symptoms can become chronic. Laboratory personnel and livestock handlers are at occupational risk.

name: Brucella
notes: Main routes of infection are consumption of raw (unpasteurized) dairy products from goats, sheep, or cattle. Also known as Malta fever, Mediterranean fever, or undulant fever. Symptoms can become chronic. Laboratory personnel and livestock handlers are at occupational risk.

description: PFAS是一类人工合成的持久性有机污染物，具有防水防油特性，广泛用于工业和消费品生产，可在环境和生物体内长期累积，并通过食物链进入人体。

llm_entry.description: PFAS是一类人工合成的持久性有机污染物，具有防水防油特性，广泛用于工业和消费品生产，可在环境和生物体内长期累积，并通过食物链进入人体。
llm_entry.name: PFAS (Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances)
llm_entry.notes: PFAS在环境中可存留数十年不易降解，全球食品贸易可能是其传播途径之一。现有研究涵盖622篇文献，来源于PubMed、Europe PMC和PubTator Central。

name: PFAS (Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances)
notes: PFAS在环境中可存留数十年不易降解，全球食品贸易可能是其传播途径之一。现有研究涵盖622篇文献，来源于PubMed、Europe PMC和PubTator Central。

description: Dioxins are persistent, bioaccumulative, and toxic environmental pollutants including polychlorinated dibenzodioxins (PCDDs) and polychlorinated dibenzofurans (PCDFs). Over 95% of human exposure occurs through food ingestion, particularly fatty foods.

llm_entry.description: Dioxins are persistent, bioaccumulative, and toxic environmental pollutants including polychlorinated dibenzodioxins (PCDDs) and polychlorinated dibenzofurans (PCDFs). Over 95% of human exposure occurs through food ingestion, particularly fatty foods.
llm_entry.name: Dioxins
llm_entry.notes: Evidence count: 610. Sources include PubMed, Europe PMC, PubTator Central, and WHO/JECFA. Often found in conjunction with PCBs. Breast milk contamination has been a concern since the 1970s. Regulated by WHO/JECFA evaluations.

name: Dioxins
notes: Evidence count: 610. Sources include PubMed, Europe PMC, PubTator Central, and WHO/JECFA. Often found in conjunction with PCBs. Breast milk contamination has been a concern since the 1970s. Regulated by WHO/JECFA evaluations.

description: Fumonisins are mycotoxins produced primarily by Fusarium moniliforme and related fungi, commonly contaminating corn and cereals. They are known to cause animal diseases including leukoencephalomalacia in horses and pulmonary edema in swine.

description: 由扩展青霉（Penicillium expansum）产生的真菌毒素，常见于霉烂苹果及苹果制品中，具有多系统毒性。

llm_entry.description: 由扩展青霉（Penicillium expansum）产生的真菌毒素，常见于霉烂苹果及苹果制品中，具有多系统毒性。

description: 硝酸盐和亚硝酸盐是天然存在的阴离子，广泛存在于食物和饮用水中。某些暴露条件下可能导致高铁血红蛋白血症等健康问题。

description: Pesticide residues refer to trace amounts of pesticides that may remain in or on food products after application. Organophosphates, a major class of pesticides, primarily affect the central nervous system by inhibiting acetylcholinesterase.

llm_entry.notes: 数据来源包括PubTator Central、FDA和WHO/JECFA；证据类型包括注释、召回记录和毒理学研究；主要涉及产品为苹果汁、婴儿食品和矿泉水。

description: 食品中残留的兽药成分，包括抗生素、激素、驱虫剂等，可能导致人体耐药性增加、过敏反应及其他健康危害。

description: 重金属污染物，通过食物链在鱼类和海鲜中蓄积，主要损害神经系统、肾脏和血液系统。

description: Giardia is a parasitic protozoan that causes giardiasis, a gastrointestinal infection commonly transmitted through contaminated food or water.

llm_entry.name: Giardia
llm_entry.notes: Based on PubTator Central annotations and CDC outbreak data. Multiple outbreaks documented including Washington 1998 (3 illnesses), New York 2000 (82 illnesses), California 2001 (34 illnesses), and Washington 2004 (19 illnesses).
